# Inflammation–SSRI Response Interaction

## Summary

Review of literature on inflammatory biomarkers as predictors of SSRI response. Three key findings with varying evidence tiers.

## Key Findings

1. CRP > 3 mg/L associated with reduced SSRI efficacy (Jha et al., 2017). E2, well-replicated across STAR*D and GENDEP cohorts.
2. [[IL6]] levels correlate with IBS-D symptom severity independent of psychiatric comorbidity (E3).
3. Baseline TNF-alpha may predict nortriptyline vs SSRI differential response (E4, single RCT).
